arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: The aftermath of Bayern Munich vs. FC Augsburg; Tottenham eyeing Jamie Gittens; Bayern scoped outside-back; Max Eberl’s future in doubt; Kevin De Bruyne leaving Manchester City; Another midfielder on Bayern’s radar; and MORE!
arts.sum: In the 42nd minute, though, Bayern Munich finally broke through when Sané whipped a pass centrally toward Musiala, where the youngster eventually corralled it and fired a shot past FC Augsburg goalkeeper Finn Dahmen (who did actually get a hand on it). FC Augsburg is a legitimate squad and Bayern Munich earned the victory, but the loss of Jamal Musiala could be a killer against Inter Milan in the Champions League.

arts.ttl: “We need everyone” — Vincent Kompany calls on Bayern Munich to step up after injury decimation
arts.sum: Looking for more thoughts and analysis on Bayern Munich’s 3-1 win over FC Augsburg? Awesome, then check out the Bavarian Podcast Works — Postgame Show!

arts.ttl: Joshua Kimmich wants resolution for Bayern Munich, Thomas Müller after FC Augsburg win
arts.sum: As Kimmich said, a resolution is needed, but might not be easy to achieve — or one where both sides come out feeling okay. Looking for more thoughts and analysis on Bayern Munich’s 3-1 win over FC Augsburg?

arts.ttl: “We have to keep going” — Bayern Munich coach Vincent Kompany on Jamal Musiala’s hamstring injury
arts.sum: Bayern Munich ’s 3-1 victory over Augsburg on Friday was marred by a potentially serious injury to attacking midfielder Jamal Musiala. Awesome, then check out the Bavarian Podcast Works — Postgame Show!

arts.ttl: Musiala joins 'crazy' Bayern Munich injury list for Inter clash
arts.sum: Jamal Musiala is set to miss the first leg of Bayern Munich's Champions League quarterfinal against Inter Milan on Tuesday, adding to the Bundesliga side's injury woes at a crucial stage of the season. "We have played without Jamal a few times already this season," said Bayern coach Vincent Kompany.

arts.ttl: European football: Bayern stretch lead while Espanyol move clear of drop
arts.sum: Harry Kane scores in 3-1 victory at 10-man Augsburg Espanyol up to 15th after 4-0 win against Rayo Vallecano Bayern Munich came back from a goal down to beat 10-man Augsburg 3-1 away on Friday and move nine points clear at the top of the Bundesliga but Jamal Musiala went off injured and looks unlikely to be fit for next week’s Champions League quarter-final. Musiala equalised before Harry Kane’s header and a deflected own goal from Augsburg’s Chrislain Matsima gave Bayern the points after the hosts took the lead thorugh Dimitris Gianoulis’ strike but ran out of steam when Cedric Zesiger was sent off. Bayern now have 68 points with six games left, nine ahead of Bayer Leverkusen, who travel to Heidenheim on Saturday. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Match Awards from Bayern Munich’s tiring 3-1 win against Augsburg
arts.sum: Leading up to what will be the most important set of match days for Bayern Munich, the Bavarians faced FC Augsburg away in the league. Though Fredrik Jensen was a solid attacking threat, the award belongs to goalkeeper Dahmen, who gave the Bavarians the hardest time…without risking a red card like Cedric Zesiger.

arts.ttl: Match Awards from Bayern Munich’s tiring 3-1 win against Augsburg

arts.ttl: Three Observations from Bayern Munich’s important 3-1 win over FC Augsburg
arts.sum: Bayern Munich will be happy to walk away with three points, but now with an injured Jamal Musiala. Jamal Musiala went down in this match with a non-contact injury.
